PLANNER / M6. WEBINAR DELIVERY PLANNER
HOW DO I GO ABOUT CREATING MY
WEBINAR SCRIPT AND PRESENTATION?
Delivering a great webinar requires:
• 	A script
• 	A presentation (slides)
YOUR SCRIPT
The best way to know what to say at each point in your webinar is to use a
base script. There are various options for creating a base script:
• 	Slide deck
• 	Slide deck with notes
• 	Slide deck with scripted sections and notes
• 	Word-for-word script from which you riff
Choose the one that fits your personal style. And use your presentation tool’s
note section to capture your script.
At minimum, we suggest you consider scripting:
✓ 	The introduction
✓ 	The pivot
✓ 	The second-level benefits
✓ 	The features
✓ 	The ‘Show the URL’
✓ 	How you’ll respond to key objections
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	3 	16
bonus tip!
Learn to read your script so it doesn’t sound
like you’re reading (in other words, practice!)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

YOUR SLIDES
When creating slides for your webinar, it’s important to:
• 	Balance words and images
• 	Create build slides
• 	Use whitespace
Balancing Words and Images
When creating your presentation, keep in mind that images are much easier
on the eyes than words. But, words better convey information. It’s about
creating a balance. Because your webinar is a selling presentation, you want
to use more images and fewer words (but make sure to use words on screen
for your key points). Images include: Videos, graphics, and other non-standard
ways of communication (e.g., boxes with arrows to indicate step-by-step
systems).
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	4 	16
bonus tip!
Offer to give attendees a copy of your
slides
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 16 --

PLANNER / M6. WEBINAR DELIVERY PLANNER
Basic formats for your slides:
• 	Image
• 	Image overlaid with text
• 	Image overlaid with text with transparency adjustments on the image
• 	½ screen text, ½ screen image
• 	Single point slides
• 	Title and bullet point slides
Creating Build Slides
When creating your slides, use build slides (google ‘create build slides in
(your presentation software)’), then look for animations. This creates change,
forcing your attendees mind back to you for every point, leaves content on
the page so they can take notes, and improves attention and retention of
data.
Using Whitespace
The message of each slide needs to be grasped within 5 seconds, otherwise,
it’s not working. So when building your slides, the fewer words on each slide,
the better. So:
✓ 	Use whitespace on your slides
✓ 	Keep it simple
✓ 	Remember that more slides make better presentations
✓ 	Don’t be afraid to kill electrons!
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	5 	16
remember…
If your message can’t be grasped within 5
seconds, it’s not working

PLANNER / M6. WEBINAR DELIVERY PLANNER
WHAT IS THE BEST WAY TO PREPARE
FOR MY WEBINAR?
There are three things you can do to effectively prepare for your webinar:
rehearsing, running a complete dress rehearsal, and physical preparation.
REHEARSING YOUR WEBINAR
Rehearsing works! You tend to get better at presenting by repetition. And
you’re not ready to take your webinar evergreen until you’ve given it 20+
times. How to rehearse your webinar:
✓ 	Start rehearsing early
✓ 	Refine every slide
✓ 	Rehearse out loud, like it’s the real thing
✓ 	Ask for feedback during each step of the performance
✓ 	Schedule dress-rehearsals (see next section) in real-world conditions
✓ 	Keep the mood light
RUNNING A DRESS REHEARSAL
The day before your webinar, do a complete dress-rehearsal live on the
platform you’ll be hosting the webinar on. All parties involved should attend
(you, your guest(s), support person(s)), with someone logging in via mobile
and someone via desktop/laptop to test the user experience.
For more on this topic, see the
Webinar Dress Rehearsal Checklist
PHYSICAL PREP FOR YOUR WEBINAR
A big piece of how much you’ll sell in your webinar has to do with how you
show up. You want to be:
✓ 	Well rested (take a nap if possible)
✓ 	Well fed (not too much)
✓ 	Well hydrated (not too much, no alcohol, have liquid with you on the
webinar)
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	7 	16
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 16 --

PLANNER / M6. WEBINAR DELIVERY PLANNER
✓ 	Well dressed with comfortable clothes and warm feet
✓ 	Well lit (place a light behind your computer monitor, shining on your face)
✓ 	Well mic’ed (high quality microphone, within 4” from your mouth, with a
pop-filter)
✓ 	Well insulated (background noise kept to a minimum to mute kids, tv,
talking, dogs)
✓ 	Well headsetted (you may need to hear people talking with you, have a
good headset handy)
✓ 	Warmed up (do vocal exercises before hand, you should always be
nervous, but never scared)
✓ 	Enthusiastically comfortable (you want to sound at your peak, but you
want to be comfortable)
In addition:
✓ 	Block off enough time
✓ 	Use a powerful computer
✓ 	Make sure you have solid bandwidth
✓ 	Use dual-monitors
Bonus tips:
✓ 	Some people do standing desks or sit at the edge of their seats
✓ 	Take a chocolate break during your webinar

PLANNER / M6. WEBINAR DELIVERY PLANNER
HOW DO I BEST DELIVER MY WEBINAR?
When getting ready to go live, there are a few things that can help you as you
get ready to deliver your webinar:
✓ 	A plan to handle questions as you present
✓ 	A plan to handle chat and questions in the sidebar
✓ 	Being prepared to handle problems as they come up
✓ 	A sidekick to support you
✓ 	An order communication system
✓ 	A way to get testimonials for your webinar
✓ 	Making sure your attendees can see you
✓ 	Using live and recorded testimonials
HANDLING QUESTIONS AS YOU PRESENT
The best description of an ideal webinar is that it’s a “controlled
conversation.” In other words, an ongoing, fluid exchange between you and
your audience. And controlled, as in you control it, but help your audience to
engage. The best way to do this is to:
✓ 	Ask your attendees to hold their questions till the end, but try to answer
them as you go along
✓ 	Learn to keep an eye on the chat
✓ 	If a question comes in that you’re going to handle later on, acknowledge
it, tell them so
✓ 	If repeat questions come in, tell them you’ll deal with it in the Q&A
✓ 	Pass along comments they make as appropriate
✓ 	Mention them by name
✓ 	Have a staff member online to answer questions
✓ 	Don’t let yourself get pulled down. If someone is chatting in a distracting
way, ignore them (you may be able to hide their chat)
✓ 	Don’t be afraid to kick out a troll (you may even have to close out chat
completely)
✓ 	Remember that you don’t need to answer every single question
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	9 	16
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 16 --

PLANNER / M6. WEBINAR DELIVERY PLANNER
HANDLING CHAT AND QUESTIONS IN THE SIDEBAR
Some webinar tools give you both a chat window and a question window.
With chat, everyone can see what someone posts. With questions, only you
can see them.
Using chat sometimes helps (it helps for community, social proof), but
sometimes hurts (it can be distracting).
Here are some tips for handling chat and questions:
✓ 	If you do use chat, be sure to turn it off during the sales offer
✓ 	The best way to handle questions in the questions box is to assign a staff
member to do it (create an internal script for them)
✓ 	Have private chat set up on your sales and checkout pages as well (set up
and staffed separately)
HANDLING PROBLEMS DURING THE WEBINAR
Many problems can be resolved in your dress rehearsal. Some of the problems
you may encounter include:
• 	Webinar program crashes
• 	Problem attendee
• 	Failed recording
• 	You make a mistake
Webinar Program Crashes
✓ 	Have everyone’s email set up in a separate campaign in your email system
✓ 	Have 2 emails written and ready to send
✓ 	Email 1
• 	“Technical difficulties"
• 	“Webinar is cancelled”
• 	“We’ll let you know shortly when we will do it”
✓ 	Email 2
• 	“Technical difficulties”
• 	“We’re jumping to backup service”
• 	“Join us, here’s the URL”
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	10 	16
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 16 --

PLANNER / M6. WEBINAR DELIVERY PLANNER
• 	"If you can’t make it, I’ll let you know when we’ll redo it”
Problem Attendee
✓ 	Kick them out
Failed Recording
✓ 	Re-record
✓ 	Have a backup
You make a mistake
✓ 	Turn it into a joke, and move on
HAVING A SIDEKICK
A sidekick can be very helpful to you as you run your webinar. This is
someone who can:
✓ 	Help in the Q&A section of the webinar
✓ 	Screen questions
✓ 	Choose the best ones
✓ 	Verbally communicate those to you
✓ 	Compile questions throughout the webinar
✓ 	Give you a testimonial
✓ 	Give you a break if you need to go to the restroom
Choose someone who knows your program, business, and customers. This
can also be the person who monitors questions throughout the webinar.
AN ORDER COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
It’s a great idea to have a communication system in place to see orders
coming in. This will give you comfort that sales are happening, and tell you
when people stop buying (so you can end the webinar).
Your order communication system could be:
• 	Your shopping cart’s reporting function
• 	Slack notifications
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	11 	16
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 16 --

PLANNER / M6. WEBINAR DELIVERY PLANNER
• 	A team member keeping a running log
✓ 	As orders come in, share with your audience verbally (not visually), and
don’t use people’s last names
GETTING TESTIMONIALS FOR YOUR WEBINAR
Getting testimonials for your webinar will help future marketing. This is
how to best capture testimonials:
✓ 	Just before the end of your webinar, ask for them. You want to
communicate:
“May I ask you a favor? I always want to improve, so I’d appreciate your
feedback. Plus, if you got value out of this webinar, I’d appreciate it if you
could give me a testimonial I could show to other people to get them to
attend. So here’s what I’d like to do. In a minute, I’m going to end this webinar.
But I’m going to let it run for about 5 more minutes. I won’t be talking, but I’ll
be here reading your comments and testimonials. If you could take a couple
of minutes and just give me feedback and/or a testimonial about the content
of this webinar, in the (chat or question) box, I’d really appreciate it.”
✓ 	Make sure you download your chat/questions at the end so you don’t lose
them
MAKE SURE THEY SEE YOU
You can build trust with your attendees by having your face on their
screen.
✓ 	Have your face on the screen the entire time (as a little window in a corner
of the screen), or
✓ 	For at least 15 minutes of your webinar, stop sharing slides, put your face
on the screen, and just talk with them. Places to do this:
✓ 	The very beginning
✓ 	At least twice during the presentation when you ask them for their
feedback and experience
✓ 	During the Q&A session
✓ 	The emotional close
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	12 	16
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

PLANNER / M6. WEBINAR DELIVERY PLANNER
LIVE AND RECORDED TESTIMONIALS
Sharing results of people who have used your product can be very
powerful. It’s one thing to have text versions. But it’s better to have them in
their own words. There are two ways to do this:
✓ 	Play a recording
✓ 	Live presentation (have those people live on the webinar to share their
story)

PLANNER / M6. WEBINAR DELIVERY PLANNER
WHEN AND HOW SHOULD I GIVE MY
AT TENDANCE BONUS?
You want to share your attendance bonus at the time during the webinar
that makes the most sense. This is usually when you’re teaching (during the
content portion of your webinar). Don’t dirty up your sales offer with it.
How to do it:
✓ 	“Remember that (thing) I promised to give you during the webinar, here it
is”
✓ 	Give a download URL
✓ 	The URL does not take them to any other page, when they click it, the file
just downloads to their computer
✓ 	Tell them not to look at it right now
When during your webinar will you deliver your attendance bonus?

PLANNER / M6. WEBINAR DELIVERY PLANNER
SHOULD I RECORD MY SALES OFFER?
You want to get your sales offer just right (this part of your webinar is vital!).
So one option is to:
✓ 	Pre-record your sales offer
✓ 	Then just hit play at that point in your webinar
✓ 	It has to be seamless (at the beginning and the end) so practice!
Will you record your sales offer?

PLANNER / M6. WEBINAR DELIVERY PLANNER
WHAT ARE THE PROS AND CONS OF
BROADCASTING TO FACEBOOK LIVE
AND/OR YOUTUBE?
Some services allow you to stream your webinar live on Facebook or
YouTube (in addition to broadcasting on your webinar platform).
You may want to do this because:
• 	Facebook/YouTube will bring additional attendees to your webinar
• 	Doing so will provide automatic alerts to your followings while you are live
• 	They also give any live broadcast recording preferential treatment (over a
regular video)
• 	As a result, you can get more viewers, including some who wouldn’t have
watched your webinar otherwise (both live and afterwards)
How to do it (from your webinar platform):
✓ 	Verify and connect to the appropriate account (you should do this before
the webinar starts)
✓ 	Select the set up screen to broadcast to either Facebook live or YouTube
✓ 	At the time you want to go live, click the button to broadcast
An alternative (that helps improve conversion rates) is to:
✓ 	Broadcast only the first 30 minutes of your webinar on Facebook or
YouTube
✓ 	Then tell people (verbally and in the comments) if they want to watch the
rest, they need to click the link to sign up and join the webinar
Will you broadcast to Facebook Live or YouTube?
